message:
✨ Add /k8s-audit-kubeconfig slash command

Adds audit command to check kubeconfig clusters and recommend cleanup.
Updates plugin version to 0.2.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+      "version": "0.2.2",

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 {
   "name": "kubectl-claude",
   "description": "AI-powered multi-cluster Kubernetes management. Discover clusters, check health, analyze RBAC, get pods/deployments/services, and troubleshoot issues across all your Kubernetes clusters.",
-  "version": "0.2.1",
+  "version": "0.2.2",
   "author": {
     "name": "KubeStellar",
     "url": "https://github.com/kubestellar/kubectl-claude"

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+---
+allowed-tools: mcp__plugin_kubectl-claude_kubectl-claude__*
+description: Audit kubeconfig clusters and recommend cleanup
+---
+
+## Your task
+
+Use the kubectl-claude MCP tools to audit all clusters in the user's kubeconfig.
+
+1. Use `audit_kubeconfig` to check connectivity to all configured clusters
+2. Present the results showing:
+   - Which clusters are accessible (with their Kubernetes versions)
+   - Which clusters are inaccessible (with error reasons)
+3. If there are inaccessible clusters, provide the cleanup commands to remove stale contexts, clusters, and users
+
+This helps users maintain a clean kubeconfig by identifying and removing stale cluster configurations.
+
+Do not use any other tools besides the kubectl-claude MCP tools.
